evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my Capacitor app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyApp.js, and shows the setup checklist (packages to install, npx cap sync, camera permission entries).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core, scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode, and npx cap sync)
Setup checklist mentions iOS Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription
Setup checklist mentions Android runtime camera permission
await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present and awaited before any other Scandit call
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
License key placeholder (e.g. YOUR_LICENSE_KEY or similar) is present
new SparkScan(settings) is present (v8 constructor used, not SparkScan.forSettings)
SparkScan.forSettings( is NOT present
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
SparkScanView.forContext( is present
sparkScan.addListener( is called to register the scan listener
Listener object has a didScan callback
Imports come from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core (DataCaptureContext, ScanditCaptureCorePlugin) and scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode (SparkScan, SparkScanView, SparkScanSettings, Symbology)

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan barcode scanning to ProductListApp.js. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedProducts array and re-render the product list.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds SparkScan to ProductListApp.js alongside the existing DOM rendering code. All existing DOM hooks, state, and render functions are preserved.

assertions.text: await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new SparkScan(settings) is present
SparkScanView.forContext( is present
sparkScan.addListener( is present
didScan callback is present
didScan callback pushes / appends to scannedProducts
Existing scannedProducts array is preserved
Existing renderProductList function is preserved and called after each scan
Existing totalLabel / total DOM update logic is preserved
Existing clearButton click handler is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Capacitor app, I want to reject it if the data is '123456789' and show an error message 'Wrong barcode' for 30 seconds before resuming scanning. All other barcodes should show success feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a feedbackDelegate object with feedbackForBarcode that returns SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback for rejected barcodes and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback for valid ones, and assigns feedbackDelegate on sparkScanView.

assertions.text: sparkScanView.feedbackDelegate is assigned
feedbackForBarcode is a method on the feedback delegate object
new SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( is returned for rejected barcodes
resumeCapturingDelay of 30 is passed to the error feedback (in seconds or ms per the API)
new SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() or SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback.fromVisualFeedbackColor is returned for valid barcodes
The rejection condition checks barcode.data === '123456789' or equivalent
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved
Imports for SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback and SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback are added

evals.prompt: I want to hide the standard SparkScan trigger button and instead use a custom HTML button in my app's UI. When the custom button is tapped, scanning should start. When tapped again while scanning, it should pause.
evals.expected_output: The skill hides the standard trigger button (triggerButtonVisible = false), wires up a custom HTML button, and implements click handlers that call sparkScanView.startScanning() and sparkScanView.pauseScanning().

assertions.text: sparkScanView.triggerButtonVisible = false is set
A custom HTML button is referenced (document.getElementById / document.querySelector)
addEventListener('click', is used to connect the button to a handler
sparkScanView.startScanning() is called in the click handler
sparkScanView.pauseScanning() is called for the pause branch
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to change the SparkScan UI look for my Capacitor app. Hide the camera switch, hide the zoom switch, and set the trigger button tint color to white.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the appropriate visibility and color properties on the sparkScanView instance using v8 property names.

assertions.text: sparkScanView.cameraSwitchButtonVisible = false OR sparkScanView.cameraSwitchControlVisible = false is set
sparkScanView.zoomSwitchControlVisible = false is set
sparkScanView.triggerButtonTintColor is assigned a Color value (Color.fromHex('#FFFFFF') or equivalent)
Color is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved
Old v6 property names (captureButtonTintColor, torchButtonVisible) are NOT present

evals.prompt: I need custom behavior when the user taps the barcode count button in the SparkScan toolbar — I want to navigate to a summary screen. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill installs a SparkScanViewUiListener on sparkScanView and implements didTapBarcodeCountButton (or the current method name) to trigger the navigation.

assertions.text: sparkScanView.uiListener is assigned an object
The uiListener object has a didTapBarcodeCountButton method
Navigation logic (e.g. location.hash change, router.push, or similar) is invoked inside the handler
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when my Capacitor view is destroyed — call dispose() on the SparkScan view so native resources are released.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires sparkScanView.dispose() into the appropriate teardown/cleanup code path, awaiting it since it is async in v8.

assertions.text: sparkScanView.dispose() is called
The dispose() call is awaited (await sparkScanView.dispose()) OR used as a promise (.then / .catch)
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to filter scans so duplicates within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my SparkScan settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the SparkScanSettings instance before creating the SparkScan mode.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 500 is set (or equivalent in the API units)
codeDuplicateFilter is set BEFORE new SparkScan(settings) is called
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan, sparkScanView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm building a high-volume warehouse scanning app where we need rapid, continuous barcode scanning without pausing between items. My app already has SparkScan integrated: IntegratedApp.js. Reconfigure it with CONTINUOUS scanning behavior and PERSISTENT preview so the camera stays visible the whole time.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures SparkScanViewSettings with defaultScanningMode set to new SparkScanScanningModeDefault(SparkScanScanningBehavior.Continuous, SparkScanPreviewBehavior.Persistent) and passes the settings as the third argument when creating the SparkScanView.

assertions.text: SparkScanViewSettings is created (new SparkScanViewSettings())
defaultScanningMode is set on the view settings
new SparkScanScanningModeDefault( is used with TWO arguments (scanning behavior AND preview behavior)
SparkScanScanningBehavior.Continuous is used (not Single)
SparkScanPreviewBehavior.Persistent is used (not Default)
The deprecated one-argument SparkScanScanningModeDefault(scanningBehavior) form is NOT used in code
The configured settings are passed to the SparkScanView creation call
Existing integration code (context, sparkScan mode, listener, lifecycle) is preserved
